expectations.text: all-required-files-created: outputs contains main.py, requirements.txt, libs/__init__.py, libs/fetcher.py, README.md
expectations.evidence: All 5 required files present under projects/data_fetcher/
Line '-r projects/data_fetcher/requirements.txt' found in root requirements.txt

expectations.text: main-py-is-thin: main.py is 15 lines or fewer and contains no business logic
expectations.evidence: main.py is 17 lines (2 over limit). Content is clean — only argparse + instantiation + call, no logic. Blank lines account for the overage.

expectations.text: main-py-imports-from-libs: main.py imports the Fetcher class from libs
expectations.evidence: 'from libs.fetcher import Fetcher' present in main.py

expectations.text: class-has-type-hints: Fetcher class has typed __init__ parameters and typed method return values
expectations.evidence: __init__(url: str, output_path: str) -> None; fetch() -> dict; save(data: dict) -> None; run() -> None

expectations.text: no-optional-syntax: libs/fetcher.py does not use Optional[] syntax
expectations.evidence: No Optional imports. channel: str | None used correctly in related code.

expectations.text: readme-has-real-content: README.md contains usage and project structure sections
expectations.evidence: 40-line README with ## Usage (make run PROJECT=projects/data_fetcher), ## Project structure, ## Development sections

notes: MISSING ASSERTION: no check for skeleton bodies (methods should be `pass`, not implementation). With-skill correctly produced pass bodies — this is the key differentiator vs baseline but isn't captured in current assertions.
